'''Craig Smith''' (born 31 October 1971<ref>{{cite news|last=Ritchie|first=Dean|title=Not being from Queensland no bar to Cane Toad State of Origin selection|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/maroons-in-a-state-of-denial/story-e6frexnr-1226034271134|accessdate=6 April 2011|newspaper=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]|date=6 April 2011|archive-date=7 July 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160707180117/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/maroons-in-a-state-of-denial/story-e6frexnr-1226034271134|url-status=live}}</ref>) is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} in the 1990s and 2000s. He played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], [[Illawarra Steelers]], [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and the [[Newcastle Knights]] as well as representing [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]], [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] and [[Queensland state rugby league team|Queensland]].

==Background==
Smith was born in [[Kaitaia]], New Zealand<ref>{{cite news|last=Ritchie|first=Dean|title=Not being from Queensland no bar to Cane Toad State of Origin selection|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/maroons-in-a-state-of-denial/story-e6frexnr-1226034271134|accessdate=6 April 2011|newspaper=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]|date=6 April 2011|archive-date=7 July 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160707180117/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/maroons-in-a-state-of-denial/story-e6frexnr-1226034271134|url-status=live}}</ref> and originally was a [[rugby union]] player in Northland. In 1988, Smith moved to [[Queensland]] to complete his final years of schooling at [https://www.ipswichgrammar.com/ Ipswich Grammar School].<ref>Revealed: Ipswich's 10 Best State of Origin Stars. https://www.thechronicle.com.au/subscribe/news/1/?sourceCode=TCWEB_WRE170_a_GGL&dest=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.thechronicle.com.au%2Fnews%2Fqueensland%2Fipswich%2Fsport%2Fipswichs-10-best-state-of-origin-players-revealed%2Fnews-story%2Fbb9bf276b6f66c61dde8b9e673c3a62c&memtype=anonymous&mode=premium</ref> He later took up [[rugby league]].<ref name=coffey>Coffey and Wood ''The Kiwis: 100 Years of International Rugby League'' {{ISBN|1-86971-090-8}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
====South Sydney Rabbitohs====
Smith commenced his top level career with the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[1995 ARL season]].

====Illawarra Steelers====
He then moved to the [[Illawarra Steelers]].

====St. George Illawarra Dragons====
Smith played at prop forward for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] in their [[1999 NRL Grand Final]] loss to the [[Melbourne Storm]].

====Wigan Warriors====
Following a series of suspensions in 2001 for contrary conduct and striking, Smith left the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] and Australia in 2002 to play for the [[Super League]] club, the [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>{{cite news|format=fee required| title=Dragons to deal directly with judiciary commissioner| date=26 April 2001| publisher=AAP Sports News (Australia)| url =http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-43722011.html| archive-url =https://web.archive.org/web/20110517011125/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-43722011.html| url-status =dead| archive-date =17 May 2011| accessdate = 27 January 2008}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|format=fee required| title=Wigan confirm Smith signing| date=17 August 2001| publisher=AAP Sports News (Australia)| url =http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-46323598.html| archive-url =https://web.archive.org/web/20110517011138/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-46323598.html| url-status =dead| archive-date =17 May 2011| accessdate = 2008-01-27}}</ref> With Wigan, he won the 2002 [[Challenge Cup]] Final. In 2003 he was named at {{rlp|pr}} in the season's [[Super League Dream Team]]. Smith played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] at {{rlp|pr}} in the [[2003 Super League Grand Final]], which was lost to the [[Bradford Bulls]].

====Newcastle Knights====
In 2005, Smith agreed to a one-year deal and a return to the NRL with the [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref>{{cite news|format=fee required|publisher=AAP Sports News (Australia)|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-106392185.html|title=Smith agrees to one-year deal with Newcastle|date=15 March 2005|accessdate=8 August 2009}}{{dead link|date=February 2019|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>

Although originally intending to retire at the end of the 2005 season, Smith was re-signed by Newcastle for the 2006 season.<ref>{{cite news|format=fee required|publisher=AAP Sports News (Australia)|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-111836734.html|title=Knights re-sign Smith and Salkeld|date=4 August 2005|accessdate=8 August 2009}}{{dead link|date=February 2019|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> A broken jaw in August 2006 sidelined him for several rounds but he was fit to return for Newcastle's finals appearances.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/league/smith-spray-as-paea-escapes-charge/2006/08/07/1154802820799.html|title=Smith spray as Paea escapes charge|date=8 August 2006|accessdate=8 August 2009|archive-date=4 November 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121104153537/http://www.smh.com.au/news/league/smith-spray-as-paea-escapes-charge/2006/08/07/1154802820799.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Smith played his last NRL game in Newcastle's semi-finals defeat by the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|publisher=The Rugby League Project|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/NRL_2006/Semi_Final/Brisbane-vs-Newcastle/summary.html|title=Brisbane Broncos 50 def. Newcastle Knights 6|accessdate=8 August 2009}}</ref>

===Representative career===
====Queensland====
Smith was selected to represent [[Queensland state rugby league team|Queensland]] as a prop for games I and II of the [[1997 State of Origin series]] and on the bench for game III.

====New Zealand====
Smith was selected to go on the [[1998 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|1998 New Zealand tour of Great Britain]] and played for the Kiwis twelve times between 1998 and 2001.

Smith was selected for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand team]] to compete in the end of season [[1999 Rugby League Tri-Nations]] tournament. In the final against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] he played at prop forward in the Kiwis' 20-22 loss.

====New Zealand Maori====
Smith was again coaxed out of retirement to take part in the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] team that met the [[Australian Aboriginal rugby league team|Indigenous Dreamtime]] team in a commemorative match at the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite news|format=fee required|publisher=AAP Sports News (Australia)|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P1-157562601.html|title=Smith coaxed out of retirement|date=24 October 2008|accessdate=8 August 2009}}{{dead link|date=February 2019|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>

==Coaching career==
Smith has been on the coaching staff for the under-20s side at [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]].<ref name="Former Knight Craig Smith ready to pull on boots at 43">{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/3181719/former-knight-craig-smith-ready-to-pull-on-boots-at-43/|title=Former Knight Craig Smith ready to pull on boots at 43|publisher=Herald|date=30 June 2015|access-date=19 April 2017}}</ref>
